title: ochna cyanophylla
intro: Ochna cyanophylla, also known as the bushwillow or forest bushwillow, is a plant species in the family Ochnaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Ochna cyanophylla include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be administered to harness its medicinal properties.
